page: Amazon Web Services m9g.12xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 microarchitecture running at 2.8 GHz. The processor configuration includes a 64 KB L1 data cache, a 2 MB L2 cache, and a 48 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps, though it does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities and memory subsystem performance. The instance achieved a strong multi-core CPU performance score of 96,265.95 in stress-ng testing, while single-core performance remains in the average tier at 2,009.19. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only cached small block speeds reaching 6,846.75 GB/sec and uncached large block reads at 187.91 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scores are in the top 10%, registering 36.49 GB/sec and 624.06 GB/sec respectively. Additionally, the platform demonstrated top-tier performance in database operations, static web serving throughput at 97.07 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations at 136.27 million operations per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARMv9-based architecture delivers high resource density and strong memory throughput relative to its general-purpose hardware class. However, workloads requiring specialized x86 instruction sets, such as AVX-512 or AMX, are unsupported due to the arm64 architecture. The absence of local storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes. Given the top-tier performance in database operations, static web serving, and compression tasks, the m9g.12xlarge is optimized for database hosting, web serving, and data processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services m9g.12xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by 48 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 cores running at 2.8 GHz on an arm64 architecture. Managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it features 192.0 GB of RAM, yielding 4.0 GB per core, and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmarks reveal strong multi-core performance and top-tier memory bandwidth, with static web serving reaching 97.07 GB/sec and Redis SET operations executing at 136.27 million operations per second. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scores also place in the top tier. While it lacks x86-specific instruction sets and local SSDs, its high memory throughput and strong database operation scores make it a strong fit for database hosting, web serving, and multi-threaded data compression workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.12xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose server with 48 dedicated Graviton ARMv9 cores, 192 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier database and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.12xlarge is an arm64 server with 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and top-tier database and web serving performance.
tagline: An arm64 general-purpose server featuring dedicated ARMv9 cores and high-throughput memory performance for database and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 cores at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers top-tier performance in database operations and Redis SET transactions
Achieves high-throughput static web serving and multi-threaded data decompression
Lacks local NVMe storage and x86-specific instruction sets like AVX-512
Optimized for database hosting, web serving, and multi-threaded data processing
